content: Just as the linguistic double meaning of reflexion (referring to both physical light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection in high school English curricula) maps to two under-explored design dimensions for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, we can frame LLM self-reflection as a process of 'bouncing' problem-solving outputs off an external 'reflective surface' (the model’s own past incorrect outputs, per the Self-Contrast framework) to generate improved corrected responses. This design mimics how physical reflection of light bounces off a surface to carry back information about the environment, rather than framing self-reflection as only an internal, abstract cognitive process.
